MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-01-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo E. Sandoval of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Campbell Industries
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Patricia Yaeger, as wrongful death heir, and as successor-in-interest to Gary Yaeger, Sr., deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAMPBELL INDUSTRIES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Campbell Industries' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Gary Yaeger, Jr. precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it is not liable under a premises liability theory. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the premises liability authority on which it relies bars plaintiffs' claims against it. Gary Yaeger, Jr.'s deposition testimony and plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden on this issue because they demonstrate that defendant's alleged liability is based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees, not on the theories discussed in defendant's authority. That is, even if defendant is correct that plaintiffs cannot demonstrate that defendant failed to warn of a concealed, pre-existing hazardous condition or negligently exercised control over decedent's work, defendant still would not demonstrate that plaintiffs cannot successfully assert their premise owner/contractor liability cause of action against it. (See Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp. (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 1315.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Natalie Ann Garcia of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.
Brenda D. Posada of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Gary Yaeger and Patricia Yaeger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is overruled. Based on the matters of which this court takes judicial notice, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' action is time-barred. While the amendment that substituted defendant for a Doe occurred after the relevant statute of limitations had run, defendant failed to demonstrate that such amendment does not relate back to the filing of the initial wrongful death action. Defendant failed to show that at that time plaintiffs knew of defendant's identity and the facts rendering it liable. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits 1, 2 and 3 and denied as to Exhibits 4, 5 and 6. However, even if the Court granted defendant's request for judicial notice in its entirety, the conclusion would be the same. Defendant must answer within 10 days. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
